fields.name: ragdoll region name#when the biped transitions to ragdoll, this region will change to the destroyed state
assassination chud text#The string id for the assassination action text in the CHUD
maximum soft landing time:seconds#the longest amount of time the biped can take to recover from a soft landing
maximum hard landing time:seconds#the longest amount of time the biped can take to recover from a hard landing
minimum soft landing velocity:world units per second#below this velocity the biped does not react when landing
minimum hard landing velocity:world units per second#below this velocity the biped will not do a soft landing when returning to the ground
maximum hard landing velocity:world units per second#the velocity corresponding to the maximum landing time
stun duration#0 is the default.  Bipeds are stunned when damaged by vehicle collisions, also some are when they take emp damage
camera interpolation start:degrees#looking-downward angle that starts camera interpolation to fp position
camera interpolation end:degrees#looking-downward angle at which camera interpolation to fp position is complete
camera offset:wu#amount of fp camera movement in (forward, right, down) when pitched down by 'camera interpolation end' above
contact points#these are the points where the biped touches the ground
reanimation character#when the flood reanimate this guy, he turns into a ...
reanimation/morph muffins#the kind of muffins I create to cover my horrible transformation
death spawn character#when I die, out of the ashes of my death crawls a ...
death particleize#optional particleization effect definition, if you want this to particleize when it dies
Player biped sound bank#Intended for biped vehicles (ie mantis)
RegenField Looping Sound#plays when player is inside a RegenField
Crouch down sound#plays when player starts crouching
Crouch up sound#plays when player stands up
Shield impact override#overrides shield impact sound, like when in a Mantis
Non shield impact override#overrides regular impact sound, like when in a Mantis
Sound radius multiplier#increases radius of other sounds when piloting this biped (mech)

velocity preservation#specifies how pre-trick velocity is maintained during and after the trick
only applies to vehicles

camera interpolation time:s#sloppiness of the camera
only applies to vehicles
trick exit time:s#how long before the end of the trick we start using the below values

trick exit camera interpolation time:s#sloppiness of the camera when exiting the trick
we interpolate between these values depending on how far your camera was displaced from the ideal camera

trick exit displacement reference:wu#when your camera is this far from the ideal at the start of the trick, we use the maximum delay time above
only for space fighter

cooldown time:s#after ending this trick, how long until I can trick again
only applies to vehicles
composition mode#if multiple markers share the same name, this specifies how to compose their search values
output function name#creates an object function with this name that you can use to drive an overlay animation
constant z offset:world units#camera z is modified by this constant z value
living material name#collision material used when character is alive
dead material name#collision material used when character is dead
dead sphere shapes*#don't be an asshole, edit something else!
pill shapes*#don't be an asshole, edit something else!
sphere shapes*#don't be an asshole, edit something else!
list sphere shapes*#don't be an asshole, edit something else!
list shapes*#don't be an asshole, edit something else!
list shape childInfos*#don't be an asshole, edit something else!
collision group override
fields.definition: This value overrides the default group, model, and rigid body.

fields.name: collision group override
fields.definition: This value overrides the default collision group.  It is overridden by any values specified rigid bodies block that overlap. Values set here can be overridden per shape through the shape primitive blocks.

fields.name: brittle timer:seconds#objects in this phantom volume will be set to brittle collision damage for this amount of time.
marker name#you don't need this if you're just generating effects.  If empty it defaults to the up of the object
alignment marker name#you don't need this if you're just generating effects.  If empty it defaults to "marker name"
Linear Motion
fields.definition:
0 - means do nothing
CENTER: motion towards marker position 
AXIS: motion towards marker axis, such that object is on the axis
DIRECTION: motion along marker direction

fields.name: hookes law e#0 if you don't want this to behave like spring.  1 is a good starting point if you do.
linear dead radius#radius from linear motion origin in which acceleration is dead.
orbit acc#negative values spin the opposite direction from positive ones
Angular Motion
fields.definition:
0 - means do nothing
ALIGNMENT: algin objects in the phantom with the marker
SPIN: spin the object about the marker axis

fields.name: alignment hookes law e#0 if you don't want this to behave like spring.  1 is a good starting point if you do.
climb inflection angle#angle for bipeds at which climb direction changes between up and down
scale airborne reaction time#scale on the time for the entity to realize it is airborne
scale ground adhesion velocity#scale on velocity with which the entity is pushed back into its ground plane (set to -1 to lock to ground)
gravity scale#scale on gravity for this entity
airborne acceleration scale#scale on airborne acceleration maximum
bank angle:degrees#angle at which we bank left/right when sidestepping or turning while moving forwards
bank apply time:seconds#time it takes us to apply a bank
bank decay time:seconds#time it takes us to recover from a bank
pitch ratio#amount that we pitch up/down when moving up or down
max velocity:world units per second#max velocity when not crouching
max sidestep velocity:world units per second#max sideways or up/down velocity when not crouching
angular velocity maximum:degrees per second#turn rate
angular acceleration maximum:degrees per second squared#turn acceleration rate
crouch velocity modifier:[0,1]#how much slower we fly if crouching (zero = same speed)
roll delay:[0,1] 1= roll fast and late
lobbing desire:1= heavy arc, 0= no arc
vaulting fields
fields.definition: The cost of the vault check is scaled by max horizontal distance divided by min object size.  Try to keep that number reasonably low.

fields.name: vault arc amount:1= heavy arc, 0= no arc
search width:wus, the side-to-side width of the search path
grab biped fields
fields.definition: Allows a biped to grab and be grabbed by other bipeds.

fields.name: grab object fields
fields.definition: Allows a biped to grab and throw crate objects.

fields.name: attach marker#The marker on the biped to which we attach the grabbed object.
ground normal dampening:react to slope changes (0=slow, 1= fast)
root offset max scale idle{root offset max scale}:vertical drop to ground allowed (0=none, 1=full)
root offset max scale moving:vertical drop to ground allowed (0=none, 1=full)
root offset dampening:react to root changes (0=slow, 1= fast)
following cam scale:root offset effect on following cam (0=none, 1=full)
stance width scale:scale pill width to use as stance radius
foot roll max:orient to ground slope (degrees)
foot pitch max:orient to ground slope (degrees)
foot pull threshold distance idle:amount past the authored plane the foot can be pulled (wu)
foot pull threshold distance moving:amount past the authored plane the foot can be pulled (wu)
foot turn minimum radius:minimum radius at which foot is fit to turn radius
foot turn maximum radius:maximum radius at which foot is fit to turn radius
foot turn threshold radius:foot is fit to turn radius fully at minimum plus threshold and above
foot turn weight dampening:dampening of fitting value for fit to turn radius(0=none, 1=fast)
foot turn blend on time:time to blend on the foot turn effect (seconds)
foot turn blend off time:time to blend off the foot turn effect (seconds)
pivot min foot delta: vert world units to find lowest foot
pivot stride length scale: leg length * this = stride length
pivot throttle scale:pivoting slows throttle (0=none, 1= full)
pivot offset dampening:react to pivot changes (0=slow, 1= fast)
pivot force turn rate:turn no matter what the pivot state is (0=control turn, 1= always turn)
ideal pelvis over high foot scale: ratio of pedestal to pelvis distance #ideal ratio of distance from the pelvis to pedestal to place pelvis over the highest foot
ideal pelvis over low foot scale: ratio of pedestal to pelvis distance #ideal ratio of distance from the pelvis to pedestal to place pelvis over the lowest foot
Pushing over Ledges
fields.definition: When a locked foot is unsupported, meaning it can't reach the ground, the biped is considered to be hanging over a ledge. You can provide a velocity that will either push the character over the ledge or away from it

fields.name: push over mag: magnitude of throttle to push over ledges. 0= no push
push back mag: magnitude of throttle to push back from ledges. 0= no push
ledgeIKSuccessRange: when unable to IK at least this close, consider the IK failed.
ledge warning time: secs to warn the player before pushing over a ledge
footlock Scale: (0-1) #how much this biped respects foot lock events
footlock Min Throttle: (0-1) #throttle at which foot lock should be fully on (footlockScale)
footlock Max Throttle: (0-1) #throttle at which foot lock should be fully off
Movement Hip Leaning
fields.definition: Allows a biped to lean based on movement.

fields.name: prediction seconds:predict ahead to determine lean. 0= off, more time=more lean
max lean angle:(degrees) maximum lean amount
max vertical dip:(fraction of leg length)
max lean angle sine:set on post-process, don't edit!*
max lean angle cosine:set on post-process, don't edit!*
Attachment Index#Sound attachment to affect - leave empty for main body
Switch point#value of the function (between 0 and 1) where the sound is triggered
Mode#0 for triggering while function is decreasing, 1 for increasing (more modes to come?)
rotation_node^:bone to rotate to align marker

options: has physical rigid bodies when alive
sync action always projects on ground

trick relative#velocity is relative to the object's orientation at the start of the trick (so if you're moving forward before the trick, you will be moving forward after the trick, even if that's a different direction)
world relative#velocity is maintained in world space

use following camera#as opposed to the trick camera, which is the default
vehicles only
do not slam player control#allows the player to continue to move aiming vector while tricking
most compressed#pick the marker that has the closest obstacle
least compressed#pick the marker that has the furthest obstacle
average#average the distances from each marker
force enable collision with player#enables collision with the player regardless of the collision group
allow feet below grade#noramlly, we will force the feet to lock to the ground surface
use biped up direction#for characters that climb walls
snap marker above contact#prevents ground marker from going below the contact point
